mechanism_narrative: The level of the unemployment rate captures aggregate labor market slack; higher unemployment reduces household income expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The correlation between UNRATE and 20-day S&P 500 forward returns is positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, labor market hysteresis erodes consumer balance sheets and triggers sustained outflows from cyclical equities, producing negative S&P 500 quarterly returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% combined with a CPI level above 210 indicates deeply negative real interest rates that force capital reallocation from cash and bonds into equities, driving positive S&P 500 monthly returns.
failure_condition: Joint observations of FEDFUNDS below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL above 210 fail to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: In the aftermath of a housing market collapse, a housing starts level above 500 thousand units signals stabilization in residential investment and construction employment, which boosts cyclical earnings and drives positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 500 during post-crisis recovery phases, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than half of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index below 95 reflects manufacturing sector contraction and negative operating leverage, which triggers rapid downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 weekly returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO is below 95.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession expansions, a real GDP level above 13000 billion chained 2017 dollars confirms aggregate demand is sufficient to sustain broad corporate revenue growth, forecasting positive S&P 500 quarterly returns.
failure_condition: Observations of GDPC1 above 13000 coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous levels of nonfarm payrolls above 130000 thousand and retail sales above 375000 million capture a virtuous employment-income-consumption loop that lifts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 weekly returns.
failure_condition: Joint observations of PAYEMS above 130000 and RSAFS above 375000 fail to generate positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% constitutes extraordinary monetary accommodation that suppresses the discount rate applied to corporate cash flows and raises equity net present values, producing positive S&P 500 monthly returns.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% are associated with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of historical observations.
